# pfsense_certificate (Resource)

Manages a [certificate](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/certificates/index.html) in the pfSense certificate manager. Supports importing external certificates for use with OpenVPN, IPsec, web GUI, and other services.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "pfsense_certificate" "webserver" {
  descr       = "Web Server Certificate"
  type        = "server"
  certificate = file("${path.module}/certs/webserver.pem")
  private_key = file("${path.module}/certs/webserver-key.pem")
}

resource "pfsense_certificate" "vpn_user" {
  descr       = "VPN User Certificate"
  type        = "user"
  certificate = file("${path.module}/certs/vpn-user.pem")
  private_key = file("${path.module}/certs/vpn-user-key.pem")
  caref       = pfsense_certificate_authority.internal_ca.refid
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `certificate` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded certificate. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `descr` (String) Descriptive name for the certificate.

### Optional

- `caref` (String) Reference ID of the signing certificate authority. Empty for self-signed or externally signed certificates.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded private key for the certificate. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `type` (String) Certificate type. Options: `server`, `user`. Defaults to `server`.

### Read-Only

- `has_private_key` (Boolean) Whether the certificate has a private key stored.
- `in_use` (Boolean) Whether the certificate is referenced by services or other configuration.
- `is_self_signed` (Boolean) Whether the certificate is self-signed.
- `issuer` (String) Certificate issuer (distinguished name).
- `refid` (String) Unique reference ID assigned by pfSense.
- `serial` (String) Certificate serial number.
- `subject` (String) Certificate subject (distinguished name).
- `valid_from` (String) Certificate validity start date.
- `valid_to` (String) Certificate validity end date.

## Import

Import is supported using the certificate's refid:

```shell
terraform import pfsense_certificate.example <refid>
```

~> **Note:** The `certificate` and `private_key` attributes cannot be read back after import and will be set to null in state. A subsequent plan may show changes if these attributes are specified in configuration.
